page_title: Tokenized Money Market Funds — Digital Assets Dashboard
page_subtitle_html: Tokenized money market fund (MMF) exposure aggregated from RWA.xyz US Treasuries and Non-U.S. Government Debt. Networks and platforms are built from each fund's on-chain token deployments; Distributed Value columns are current levels.
kpi_window_note: Distributed value uses a 30-day (30D) % change vs summed token values 30 days ago. 30D net change is the dollar change in total distributed value across all funds in the same window. Fund universe: tokenized money market funds on RWA.xyz US Treasuries and Non-U.S. Government Debt pages.

bottom_cta.label: See US Treasuries on RWA.xyz (MMF listings)

key_observations_html:
• TMMFs are becoming a de facto settlement layer for institutions: several of the largest funds combine very high AUM with small holder counts—e.g. USYC (44 holders, $2.98B; ~$68M/holder), iBENJI (27 holders, $1.60B; ~$59M/holder), BUIDL (109 holders, $2.42B; ~$22M/holder)—suggesting on-chain cash and collateral rails rather than broad retail savings products. Ethereum still carries ~42% of aggregated MMF network share, alongside selective deployment on chains used for institutional workflows.
  • Early "flight to efficiency" shows up in 30D network share: gainers include Solana (+2.29 pp 30D share), Plume (+2.07 pp 30D share); losers include BNB Chain (-5.59 pp 30D share), Stellar (-0.91 pp 30D share). That pattern is consistent with liquidity migrating toward higher-throughput, lower-fee chains—though Ethereum still dominates levels in most snapshots.
  • Industry headlines emphasize Issuer strategy split Recent coverage includes Coinbase brings global crypto derivatives markets to US institutional clients (CoinTelegraph); Buy $72K dip, or jump ship: What will Bitcoin bulls do? (CoinTelegraph). Issuer strategy coverage can clarify whether the market is bifurcating into institutional rails vs retail yield products—holder counts and AUM per holder in the funds table help validate that split.
  • Industry headlines emphasize Multi-chain distribution Recent coverage includes Mass deployment of AI agents is a disaster waiting to happen, says CertiK CEO (CoinDesk); DTCC Picks Stellar for Tokenized Securities Rollout as Multi-Chain Push Expands (The Defiant). Multi-chain deployment headlines often reflect compliance and distribution requirements rather than deep liquidity on every chain—compare with funds that list many networks but very few holders.
  • Industry headlines emphasize Regulation & compliance Recent coverage includes Clarity Act Risks Regulation Without Oversight, Brookings Fellow Says (CoinDesk); Paxos wins SEC approval to clear U.S. stocks on blockchain (CoinDesk). Cross-border compliance stories often explain why similar funds sit under different regulatory labels—jurisdiction and investor-eligibility columns on this page are the practical read-through.

Context only—not investment advice. Observations rank fund-level and network aggregates on this page against recent tokenization and money-market headlines. Bullets are ranked from on-page data and recent industry headlines (dashboard RSS plus Google News fallback).

Key observations are AI generated and should be reviewed for accuracy.

funds_table.search_placeholder: Filter by fund name, ticker, platform, network, domicile…

networks.caption_html: Networks table sums distributed value across tokenized money market fund token deployments on each chain. 7D Δ value and market share are computed from aggregated token rows (RWA.xyz US Treasuries and Non-U.S. Government Debt fund lists).
networks.chart_note_html: The chart lists the top 12 networks by total value (labels include market share). Scroll the table for the full filtered list.
networks.section_intro_html: Networks — aggregate distributed value of tokenized money market funds by chain, summed from each fund's token deployments.

platforms.caption_html: Platforms table groups the same fund universe by asset manager (issuer). RWA Count is the number of distinct MMF products with exposure for that manager.
platforms.chart_note_html: The chart lists the top 12 platforms by total value (labels include market share). Scroll the table for the full filtered list.
platforms.section_intro_html: Platforms — issuer / asset-manager aggregates for tokenized MMFs (same measures as Networks).
